entities.name: PHYTOREMEDIATION
entities.description: A plant-driven process for remediating contaminated soil and water by taking up contaminants including potentially toxic elements

entities.name: CONTAMINATED SOIL
entities.description: Soil containing potentially toxic elements that requires remediation

entities.name: CONTAMINATED WATER
entities.description: Water containing potentially toxic elements that requires remediation

entities.name: POTENTIALLY TOXIC ELEMENTS
entities.description: Contaminants including metals and metalloids that are removed during phytoremediation

entities.name: POTENTIALLY TOXIC METALS
entities.description: Toxic metallic elements targeted for removal via phytoremediation

entities.name: METALLOIDS
entities.description: Toxic non-metal elements targeted for removal via phytoremediation

entities.name: METALLOPHYTES
entities.description: Plants capable of growing in and accumulating metals from the soil

entities.name: WILD HERBACEOUS SPECIES
entities.description: Non-woody plants used as metallophytes for phytoremediation

entities.name: TREES
entities.description: Woody perennial plants used as metallophytes for phytoremediation

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL CROPS
entities.description: Cultivated plants used as metallophytes for phytoremediation

entities.name: EUROPE
entities.description: The geographic region where the phytoremediation of anthropogenically influenced soils is focused

entities.name: BIOMASS UTILISATION
entities.description: The process of managing and using contaminated plant material after phytoremediation

entities.name: METAL STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The physiological ability of plants to withstand high concentrations of toxic metals

entities.name: AMENDMENTS
entities.description: Substances added to soil to enhance the efficiency of phytoremediation

entities.name: SYNERGISTIC ENHANCING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Combined techniques used to improve the effectiveness of phytoremediation
